ogTitle: Horseshoe Lagoon vs Horton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Horseshoe Lagoon and Horton.
heading: Horseshoe Lagoon vs Horton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Horseshoe Lagoon, Queensland and Horton, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Horseshoe Lagoon has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Horton (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Horseshoe Lagoon or Horton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Horseshoe Lagoon has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Horton scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Horseshoe Lagoon scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Horton scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Horseshoe Lagoon or Horton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Horseshoe Lagoon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.68% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
